mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. An object at rest will stay at rest unless acted on by a force.
B. Force equals mass times acceleration.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. Energy cannot be created or destroyed.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of inertia?
options: A. A car accelerating on a highway.
B. A book resting on a table.
C. A rocket launching into space.
D. A person pushing a swing.

mcqs.question: In the absence of a net force, an object's velocity will:
options: A. Change.
B. Remain constant.
C. Decrease.
D. Become zero.

mcqs.question: If a hockey puck slides on ice and eventually stops, what has acted on it?
options: A. Gravity.
B. Friction.
C. Air resistance.
D. Both B and C.

mcqs.question: Which of the following describes the concept of inertia?
options: A. The tendency of a body to resist changes in its state of motion.
B. The force required to change the motion of an object.
C. The mass of an object in motion.
D. The speed of an object in a straight line.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: An object at rest tends to stay at rest, while an object in motion tends to stay in motion unless acted upon by a ______.
The property of a body to resist changes in its state of motion is called ______.
Newton's First Law is also known as the Law of ______.
The greater the mass of an object, the greater its ______.
In the absence of a net force, an object's velocity will remain ______.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
Give an example of inertia in daily life.
Why do we wear seatbelts in cars?
What happens to a ball thrown in space?
Explain how Newton's First Law applies to a book on a table.
